author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter V. 19–40.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a certain place in the Gospel, the Lord says that the prudent hearer of His word ought to be like a man who, wishing to build a house, digs deeply until he comes to the foundation of stability on the rock, and there establishes in security what he builds against the violence of the flood; so that, when the flood comes, it may be rather beaten back by the strength of the building. than bring ruin on that house by the force of its pressure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us regard the Scripture of God to be, as it were, the field where we wish to build something.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not be slothful, nor be content with the surface; let us dig deeply until we come to the rock: “And that rock was Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The passage read to-day has spoken to us of the witness of the Lord, that He does not hold the witness of men necessary, but has a greater witness than men; and He has told us what this witness is: “The works,” saith He, “which I do bear witness of me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then He added, “And the Father that sent me beareth witness of me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The very works also which He doeth, He says that He has received from the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The works, therefore, bear witness, the Father bears witness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has John borne no witness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did clearly bear witness, but as a lamp; not to satisfy friends, but to confound enemies: for it had been predicted long before by the person of the Father, “I have prepared a lamp for mine Anointed: I will clothe His enemies with confusion; but upon Him shall flourish my sanctification.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be it that thou wert left in the dark in the night-time, thou didst direct thy attention to the lamp, thou didst admire the lamp, and didst exult at its light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that lamp says that there is a sun, in which thou oughtest to exult; and though it burns in the night, it bids thee to be looking out for the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore it is not the case that there was no need of that man’s testimony.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For wherefore was he sent, if there was no need of him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, on the contrary, lest man should stay at the lamp, and think the light of the lamp to be sufficient for him, therefore the Lord neither says that this lamp had been superfluous, nor yet doth He say that thou oughtest to stay at the lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Scripture of God utters another testimony: there undoubtedly God hath borne witness to His Son, and in that Scripture the Jews had placed their hope,—namely, in the law of God, given by Moses His servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Search the Scripture,” saith He, “in which ye think ye have eternal life: the same bears witness of me; and ye will not come to me that ye may have life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why do ye think that in the Scripture ye have eternal life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask itself to whom does it bear witness, and understand what is eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because for the sake of Moses they were willing to reject Christ, as an adversary to the ordinances and precepts of Moses, He convicts those same men as by another lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, indeed, all men are lamps, since they can be both lighted and extinguished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, when the lamps are wise, they shine and glow with the Spirit; yet also, if they did burn and are put out, they even stink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The servants of God remain good lamps by the oil of His mercy, not by their own strength.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The free grace of God, truly, is the oil of the lamps.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For I have labored more than they all,” saith a certain lamp; and lest he should seem to burn by his own strength, he added, “But not I, but the grace of God that was with me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All prophecy, therefore, before the coming of the Lord, is a lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of this lamp the Apostle Peter says: “We have a more sure word of prophecy, to which ye do well giving heed, as unto a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the day-star arise in your hearts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly the prophets are lamps, and all prophecy one great lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What of the apostles?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are not they, too, lamps?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are, clearly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He alone is not a lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He is not lighted and put out; because “even as the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostles also, I say, are lamps; and they give thanks because they were both lighted by the light of truth, and are burning with the spirit of charity, and supplied with the oil of God’s grace.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they were not lamps, the Lord would not say to them, “Ye are the light of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after He said, “Ye are the light of the world,” He shows that they should not think themselves such a light as that of which it is said, “That was the true light, that enlighteneth every man coming into this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this was said of the Lord at that time when He was distinguished from John (the Baptist).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of John the Baptist, indeed, it had been said, “He was not the light, but that he might bear witness of the light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And lest thou shouldst say, How was he not the light, of whom Christ says that “he was a lamp”?—I answer, In comparison of the other light, he was not light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “that was the true light that enlighteneth every man coming into this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, when He said also to the disciples, “Ye are the light of the world,” lest they should imagine that anything was attributed to them which was to be understood of Christ alone, and thus the lamps should be extinguished by the wind of pride, when He had said, “Ye are the light of the world,” He immediately subjoined, “A city that is set on a hill cannot be hid; neither do men light a candle and put it under a bushel, but an a candlestick, that it may shine on all that are in the house.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what if He did not call the apostles the candle, but the lighters of the candle, which they were to put on a candlestick?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear that He called themselves the candle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “So let your light shine,” saith He, “before men, that they, seeing your good works, may glorify,” not you, but “your Father who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore both Moses bore witness to Christ, and John bore witness to Christ, and all the other prophets and apostles bore witness to Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before all these testimonies He places the testimony of His own works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because through those men too, it was God and none other that bore witness to His Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet in another way God bears testimony to His Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God reveals His Son through the Son Himself, He reveals Himself through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To Him, if a man shall have been able to reach, he shall need no lamps; and by truly digging deep, he will carry down his building to the rock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The lesson of to-day, brethren, is easy; but on account of what was due yesterday (for I know what I have delayed, not withdrawn, and the Lord has deigned to allow me even to-day to speak to you), recall to mind what you ought to demand, if perhaps, while preserving piety and wholesome humility, we may in some measure stretch out ourselves, not against God, but towards Him, and lift up our soul, pouring it out above us, like the Psalmist, to whom it was said, “Where is thy God?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “On these things,” saith he, “I meditated, and poured out my soul above me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore let us lift up our soul to God, not against God; for this also is said, “To Thee, O Lord, I have lifted up my soul.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And let us lift it up with His own assistance, for it is heavy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And from what cause is it heavy?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the body which is corrupt weighs down the soul, and the earthly tabernacle depresses the mind while meditating on many things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us try, then, whether we may not be able to withdraw our mind from many things in order to concentrate it on one, and to raise it to one (which indeed we cannot do, as I have said, unless He assist us who wills our souls to be raised to Himself).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so we may apprehend in some measure how the Word of God, the only begotten of the Father, the co-eternal and equal with the Father, doeth not anything except what He seeth the Father doing, whilst yet the Father Himself doeth not anything but through the Son, who seeth Him doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since the Lord Jesus, as it seems to me,—willing here to make known some great matter to those that give attention to it, and to pour into those that are capable of receiving, and to rouse, on the other hand, the incapable to assiduity, in order that, while not yet understanding, they may by right living be made capable,—has intimated to us that the human soul and rational mind which is in man, not in the beast, is invigorated, enlightened, and made happy in no other way than by the very substance of God: that the soul itself gets somewhat by and of the body, and yet holds the body subject to it, while the senses of the body can be soothed and delighted by things bodily, and that because of this kind of fellowship of soul and body in this life, and in this mutual embrace of theirs, the soul is delighted when the bodily senses are soothed, and saddened when they are offended; while yet the happiness by which the soul itself is made happy cannot be realized but by a participation of that ever-living, unchangeable life, of that eternal substance, which is God: that as the soul, which is inferior to God, causes the body, which is inferior to itself, to live, so that alone which is superior to the soul can cause that same soul to live happily.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the soul is higher than the body, and higher than the soul is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It bestows something on its inferior, while there is something bestowed on itself by the superior.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it serve its Lord, that it may not be trampled on by its own servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, brethren, is the Christian religion, which is preached through the whole world, while its enemies are dismayed; who, where they are conquered, murmur, and fiercely rage against it where they prevail.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the Christian religion, that one God be worshipped, not many gods, because only one God can make the soul happy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is made happy by participation of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not by participation of a holy soul does the feeble soul become happy, nor by participation of an angel does the holy soul become happy; but if the feeble soul seeks to be happy, let it seek that by which the holy soul is made happy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou art made happy, not of an angel, but the angel as well as thou of the same source.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These things being premised and firmly established,—that the rational soul is made happy only by God, that the body is enlivened only by the soul, and that the soul is a something intermediate between God and the body,—direct your thoughts to, and recollect with me, not the passage read to-day, of which we have spoken enough, but that of yesterday, which we have been turning over and handling these three days, and, to the best of our abilities, digging into until we should come to the rock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word Christ, Christ the Word of God with God, Christ the Word and the Word God, Christ and God and Word one God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To this press on; O soul, despising, or even transcending all things else, to this press on.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is nothing more powerful than this creature, which is called the rational mind, nothing more sublime: whatever is above this, is but the Creator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I was saying that Christ is the Word, and Christ is the Word of God, and Christ the Word is God; but Christ is not only the Word, since “the Word became flesh, and dwelt among us:” therefore Christ is both Word and flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when “He was in the form of God, He thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what of us in our low estate, who, feeble and crawling on the ground, were not able to reach unto God, were we to be abandoned?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He emptied Himself, taking upon Him the form of a servant;” not, therefore, by losing the form of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He became man who was God, by receiving what He was not, not by losing what He was: so God became man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There thou hast something for thy weakness, something for thy perfection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let Christ raise thee by that which is man, lead thee by that which is God-man, and guide thee through to that which is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the whole preaching and dispensation by Christ is this, brethren, and there is not another, that souls may be raised again, and that bodies also may be raised again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For each of the two was dead; the body by weakness, the soul by iniquity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because each was dead, each may rise again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Soul and body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By what, then, can the soul rise again but by Christ God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By what the body, but by the man Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there was also in Christ a human soul, a whole soul; not merely the irrational part of the soul, but also the rational, which is called mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there have been certain heretics, and they have been driven out of the Church, who fancied that the body of Christ did not have in it a rational mind, but, as it were, the animal life of a beast; since, without the rational mind, life is only animal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because they were driven out, and driven out by the truth, accept thou the whole Christ, Word, rational mind, and flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the whole Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let thy soul rise again from iniquity by that which is God, thy body from corruption by that which is man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There, most beloved, hear ye what, so far as it appears to me, is the great profundity of this passage; and see how Christ here speaks to the effect, that the only reason why He came is, in order that souls may have a resurrection from iniquity, and bodies from corruption.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have already said by what our souls are raised, by the very substance of God; by what our bodies are raised, by the human dispensation of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing; for what things soever He has done, these also the Son doeth in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yes, the heaven, the earth, the sea; the things that are in heaven, on the earth, and in the sea; the visible and invisible, the animals on the land, the plants in the fields, the creatures that swim in the waters, that fly in the air, that shine in heaven; besides all these, angels, virtues, thrones, dominations, principalities, powers; “all were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did God make all these, and show them when made to the Son, that He also should make another world full of all these?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, on the contrary, what does He say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For what things soever He has made, these,” not others, but “these also the Son doeth,” not differently, “but in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the Father loveth the Son, and showeth Him all things which Himself doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father showeth to the Son that souls may be raised, for souls are raised up by the Father and the Son; nor can souls live except God be their life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If souls, then, cannot live unless God be their life, just as themselves are the life of bodies; what the Father shows to the Son, that is, what He doeth, He doeth through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is not by doing that He shows to the Son, but by showing He doeth through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Son sees the Father showing before anything is done; and from the Father’s showing and the Son’s vision, is done what is done by the Father through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So are souls raised up, if they can see that conjunction of unity, the Father showing, the Son seeing, and the creature made by the Father’s showing and the Son’s seeing; and that thing made by the Father’s showing and the Son’s seeing, which is neither the Father nor the Son, but beneath the Father and the Son, whatever is made by the Father through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who sees this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, again we humble ourselves to carnal notions, and descend to you, if indeed we had at any time ascended somewhat from you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wishest to show something to thy son, that he may do what thou doest; thou art about to do, and thus to show the thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, what thou art about to do, in order to show it to thy son, thou doest not surely by thy son; but thou alone doest that thing which, when done, he may see, and do another such thing in like manner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is not the case there; why goest thou on to thy own similitude, and blottest out the similitude of God within thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There, the case is wholly otherwise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Find a case in which thou showest to thy son what thou doest before thou doest it; so that, after thou hast shown it, it will be by the son thou doest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps something like this now occurs to thee: Lo, sayest thou, I think to make a house, and I wish it to be built by my son: before I build it myself, I point out to my son what I mean to do: both he doeth, and I too by him to whom I pointed out my wish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast retreated, indeed, from the former similitude, but still thou liest in great dissimilitude.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, lo, before thou canst make the house, thou dost inform thy son, and point out to him what thou meanest to do; that, upon thy showing before thou makest, he may make what thou hast shown, and so thou mayest make by him: but thou wilt speak words to thy son, words will have to pass between thee and him; between the person showing and the person seeing, between speaker and hearer, flies articulate sound, which is not what thou art, nor what he is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That sound, indeed, which goes out of thy mouth, and by the concussion of the air touches thy son’s ear, and filling the sense of hearing, conveys thy thought to his heart; that sound, I say, is not thyself, nor thy son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A sign is given from thy mind to thy son’s mind, but that sign not either thy mind or thy son’s mind, but something else.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it thus that we think the Father has spoken to the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Were there words between the Father and the Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then how is it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or, whatever the Father would say to the Son, if He would say it by a word, the Son Himself is the Word of the Father, would He speak by a word to the Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or, since the Son is the great Word, had smaller words to pass between the Father and Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it so, that some sound, as it were a temporal, fleeting creature, had to issue from the mouth of the Father, and strike upon the ear of the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has God a body, that this should proceed, as it were, from His lips?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And has the Word the ears of a body, into which sound may come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lay aside all notions of corporeal forms, regard simplicity, if thou art single-minded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how wilt thou be single-minded?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou wilt not entangle thyself with the world, but disentangle thyself from the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by disentangling thyself, thou wilt be single-minded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And see, if thou canst, what I say; or if thou canst not, believe what thou dost not see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou speakest to thy son; thou speakest by a word: neither art thou, nor is thy son, the word that sounds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have, sayest thou, another method of showing; for so well instructed is my son, that he hears without my speaking, but I show him by a nod what to do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lo, show him by a nod what thou wilt, yet certainly the mind holds within itself that which it would show.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By what dost thou give this nod?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With the body,—namely, with the lips, the look, the brows, the eyes, the hands.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All these are not what thy mind is: these, too, are media; there was something understood by these signs which are not what thy mind is, not what the mind of thy son is; but all this which thou doest by the body is beneath thy mind, and beneath the mind of thy son: nor can thy son know thy mind, unless thou give him signs by the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, do I say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is not the case there; there all is simplicity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father shows to the Son what He is doing, and by showing begets the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I see what I have said; but because I see also to whom I have said it, may such understanding be some time or other formed in you as to grasp it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If ye are not able now to comprehend what God is, comprehend at least what God is not: you will have made much progress, if you think of God as being not something other than He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God is not a body, not the earth, not the heaven, not the moon, or sun, or stars—not these corporeal things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if not heavenly things, how much less is He earthly things!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Put all body out of the question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Further, hear another thing: God is not a mutable spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I confess,—and it must be confessed, for it is the Gospel that speaks it,—“God is a Spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But pass beyond all mutable spirit, beyond all spirit that now knows, now knows not; that now remembers, now forgets; that wills what before it willed not, that wills not what before it willed; either that suffers these mutabilities now or may suffer them: pass beyond all these.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou findest not any mutability in God; nor aught that may have been one way before, and is otherwise now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For where thou findest alternation, there a kind of death has taken place: since, for a thing not to be what it was, is a death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul is said to be immortal; so indeed it is, because it ever lives, and there is in it a certain continuous life, but yet a mutable life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: According to the mutability of this life, it may be said to be mortal; because if it lived wisely, and then becomes foolish, it dies for the worse; if it lived foolishly, and becomes wise, it dies for the better.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Scripture teaches us that there is a death for the worse, and that there is a death for the better.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In any case, they had died for the worse, of whom it said, “Let the dead bury their dead;” and, “Awake, thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light;” and from this passage before us, “When the dead shall hear, and they that hear shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the worse they had died; therefore do they come to life again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By coming to life they die for the better, because by coming to life again they will not be what they were; but for that to be, which was not, is death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps it is not called death if it is for the better?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle has called that death: “But if ye be dead with Christ from the elements of this world, why do ye judge concerning this world as if ye were still living?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And again, “For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He wishes us to die that we may live, because we have lived to die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever therefore dies, both from better to worse, and from worse to better, is not God; because neither can supreme goodness proceed to better, nor true eternity to worse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For true eternity is, where is nothing of time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But was there now this, now that?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Immediately time is admitted, it is not eternal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that ye may know that God is not thus, as the soul is,—certainly the soul is immortal,—what, however, saith the apostle of God, “Who alone hath immortality,” unless that he openly says this, He alone hath unchangeableness, because He alone hath true eternity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore no mutability is there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Recognize in thyself something which I wish to say within, in thyself; not within as if in thy body, for in a sense one may say, “in thyself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there is in thee health, thy age whatever it be, but this in regard to the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In thee is thy hand and thy foot; but there is one thing in thee, within; another thing in thee as in thy garment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But leave outside thy garment and thyself, descend into thyself, go to thy secret place, thy mind, and there see, if thou canst, what I wish to say.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if thou art far from thyself, how canst thou come near to God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I was speaking of God, and thou believedst that thou wouldst understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am speaking of the soul, I am speaking of thyself: understand this, there I will try thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I do not travel very far for examples, when I mean to give thee some similitude to thy God from thy own mind; because surely not in the body, but in that same mind, was man made after the image of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us seek God in His own similitude; let us recognize the Creator in His own image.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There within, if we can, let us find this that we speak of,—how the Father shows to the Son, and how the Son sees what the Father shows, before anything is made by the Father through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when I shall have spoken, and thou hast understood, thou must not think that spoken of to be something just such as our example, that thou mayest therein keep piety, which I wish to be kept by thee, and earnestly admonish thee to keep: that is, if thou art not able to comprehend what God is, do not think it a small matter for thee to know what He is not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, in thy mind, I see some two things, thy memory and thy thought, which is, as it were, the seeing faculty and the vision of thy soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou seest something, and perceivest it by the eyes, and thou committest it to the care of the memory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There, within, is that which thou hast committed to thy memory, laid up in secret as in a storehouse, as in a treasury, as in a kind of secret chamber and inner cabinet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou thinkest of something else, thy attention is elsewhere; what thou didst see is in thy memory, but not seen by thee, because thy thought is bent on another thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I prove this at once.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I speak to you who know; I mention by name Carthage; all who know it have instantly seen Carthage within the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are there as many Carthages as there are minds of you?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have all seen it by means of this name, by means of these syllables known to you, rushing forth from my mouth: your ears were touched; the sense of the soul was touched through the body, and the mind bent back from another object to this word, and saw Carthage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was Carthage made there and then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was there already, but latent in the memory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why was latent there?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because thy mind was engaged on another matter; but when thy thought turned back to that which was in the memory, thence it was shaped, and became a kind of vision of the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before, there was not a vision, but there was memory; the vision was made by the turning back of thought to memory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy memory, then, showed Carthage to thy thought; and that which was in it before thou didst direct thy mind to the memory, it exhibited to the attention of thy thought when turned upon it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, a showing is effected by the memory, and a vision is produced in thought; and no words passed between, no sign was given from the body: thou didst neither nod, nor write, nor utter a sound; and yet thought saw what the memory showed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But both that which showed, and that to which it showed, are of the same substance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet, that thy memory might have Carthage in it, the image was drawn in through the eyes, for thou didst see what thou didst store up in thy memory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So hast thou seen the tree which thou rememberest; so the mountain, the river; so the face of a friend, of an enemy, of father, mother, brother, sister, son, neighbor; so of letters written in a book, of the book itself; so of this church: all these thou didst see, and didst commit to thy memory after they were seen; and didst, as it were, lay up there what thou mightst by thinking see at will, even when they should be absent from these eyes of the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou sawest Carthage when thou wast at Carthage; thy soul received the image by the eyes; this image was laid up in thy memory; and thou, the person who wast present at Carthage, didst keep something within thee which thou mightst be able to see with thyself, even when thou shouldst not be there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All these things thou didst receive from without.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What the Father shows to the Son, He does not receive from without: all comes to pass within, because there would be no creature at all without, unless the Father had made it by the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Every creature was made by God; before it was made it was not in being.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was not therefore seen, after being made and retained in memory, that the Father might show it to the Son, as the memory might show to thought; but, on the contrary, the Father showed it to be made, the Son saw it to be made; and the Father made it by showing, because He made it by the Son seeing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And therefore we ought not to be surprised that it is said, “But what He seeth the Father doing,” not showing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by this it is intimated that, with the Father, to do and to show is the same thing; that hence we may understand that He doeth all things by the Son seeing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Neither is that showing, nor that seeing, temporal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Forasmuch as all times are made by the Son, they could not certainly be shown to Him at any point of time to be made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Father’s showing begets the Son’s seeing, just in the same manner as the Father begets the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the showing produces the seeing, not the seeing the showing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if we were able to look into this matter more purely and perfectly, perhaps we should find that the Father is not one thing, His showing another; nor the Son one thing, His seeing another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if we have hardly apprehended this,—if we have hardly been able to explain how the memory exhibits to the thought what it has received from without,—how much less can we take in or explain how God the Father shows to the Son, what He has not from elsewhere, or that which is not other than Himself!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are only little ones: I tell you what God is not, do not show you what God is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall we do, then, that we may apprehend what He is?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Can ye do this by or through me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I say this to the little ones, both to you and to myself; there is by whom we can: we have just now sung, just now heard, “Cast thy care upon the Lord, and He will nourish thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The reason why thou art not able, O man, is because thou art a little one; being a little one, thou must be nourished; being nourished, thou wilt become full-grown; and what as a little one thou couldst not, thou shalt see when full-grown; but that thou mayest be nourished, “cast thy care upon the Lord, and He will nourish thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore let us now briefly run over what remains, and do you see how the Lord makes known to us the things which I have been here commending to your attention.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Father loveth the Son, and showeth Him all things which Himself doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Himself raiseth up souls, but by the Son, that the souls raised up may enjoy the substance of God, that is, of the Father and of the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And greater works than these He will show Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Greater than which?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Than healings of bodies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have treated of this already, and must not linger upon it now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Greater is the resurrection of the body unto eternity than this healing of the body, wrought in that impotent man, to last only for a time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And greater works than these He will show Him, that ye may marvel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Will show,” as if the act were temporal, therefore as to a man made in time, since God the Word is not made, He by whom all times were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Christ was made man in time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We know in what consulship the Virgin Mary brought forth Christ, conceived of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He, by whom as God the times were made, was made man in time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, just as in time, “He will show Him greater works,” that is, the resurrection of bodies, “that ye may marvel” at the resurrection of bodies wrought by the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He then returns to that resurrection of souls: “For as the Father raiseth the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will;” but this according to the Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father quickeneth, the Son quickeneth; the Father whom He will, the Son whom He will; but the Father quickeneth the same as the Son, because all things were made by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is said of the resurrection of souls; but what of the resurrection of bodies?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He returns, and says: “For the Father judgeth not any man, but all judgment hath He given to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The resurrection of souls is effected by the eternal and unchangeable substance of the Father and Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the resurrection of bodies is effected by the dispensation of the Son’s humanity, which dispensation is temporal, not co-eternal with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, when He mentioned judgment, in which there should be a resurrection of bodies, He saith, “For the Father judgeth not any man, but all judgment hath He given to the Son;” but concerning the resurrection of souls, He saith, “Even as the Father raiseth the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That, then, the Father and the Son together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this concerning the resurrection of bodies: “The Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son; that all may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is referred to the resurrection of souls.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That all may honor the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Even as they honor the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Son works the resurrection of souls in the same manner as the Father doth; the Son quickeneth just as the Father doth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, in the resurrection of souls, “let all honor the Son as they honor the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what of the honoring on account of the resurrection of the body?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whoso honoreth not the Son, honoreth not the Father that sent Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not even as, but honoreth and honoreth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the man Christ is honored, but not even as God the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because, with respect to this, He said, “The Father is greater than I.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when is the Son honored even as the Father is honored?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God; and all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And hence, in this second honoring, what saith He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whoso honoreth not the Son, honoreth not the Father that sent Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son was not sent, but because He was made man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again He returns to the resurrection of souls, that by continual repetition we may apprehend His meaning; because we could not keep up with His discourse hastening on as on wings.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lo, the Word of God lingers with us; lo, it doth, as it were, dwell with our infirmities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He returns again to the mention of the resurrection of souls.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whoso heareth my word, and believeth Him that sent me, hath eternal life;” but hath it as from the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For whoso heareth my word, and believeth Him that sent me, hath eternal life” from the Father, by believing the Father that sent the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And shall not come into judgment, but is passed from death to life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But from the Father, whom he believes, is he quickened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, dost Thou not quicken?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See that the Son also “quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, That the hour cometh when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God, and they that hear shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here He did not say, they shall believe Him that sent me, and therefore shall live; but by hearing the voice of the Son of God, “they that hear,” that is, they that obey the Son of God, “shall live.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, both from the Father shall they live, when they will believe the Father; and from the Son shall they live, when they will hear the voice of the Son of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why shall they live both from the Father and from the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For even as the Father hath life in Himself, so also hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has finished speaking of the resurrection of souls; it remains to speak more evidently of the resurrection of bodies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And hath given Him authority also to execute judgment:” not only to raise up souls by faith and wisdom, but also to execute judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because He is the Son of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the Father doeth something through the Son of man, which He doeth not from His own substance, to which the Son is equal: as, for instance, that He should be born, crucified, dead, and have a resurrection; for not any of these is contingent to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the same manner also the raising again of bodies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the raising to life of souls the Father effects from His own substance, by the substance of the Son, in which the Son is equal to Him; because souls are made partakers of that unchangeable light, but not bodies; but the raising again of bodies, the Father effects through the Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “He hath given Him authority also to execute judgment, because He is the Son of man;” according to that which He said above, “For the Father judgeth not any man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to show that He said this of the resurrection of bodies, He goes on: “Marvel not at this, for the hour cometh:” not, and now is; but, “the hour cometh, in which all that are in the graves (this ye have already heard sufficiently explained yesterday) shall hear His voice, and come forth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Into judgment: “They that have done well, into the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, into the resurrection of judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And dost Thou do this alone, because the Father hath given all judgment to the Son, and judgeth not any man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I, saith He, do it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how doest Thou it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I cannot of myself do anything; as I hear, I judge; and my judgment is just.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He was treating of the resurrection of souls, He did not say, I hear; but, I see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I hear refers to the command of the Father as giving order.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, now as a man, just as He than whom the Father is greater; as from the form of a servant, not from the form of God, “As I hear, I judge; and my judgment is just.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence is the man’s judgment a just one?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, mark well: “Because I seek not my own will, but the will of Him that sent me.”
